title: senecio teixeirae
intro: Senecio teixeirae, also known as the Brazilian ragwort, is a plant species within the daisy family. Traditionally used for its medicinal properties, the roots and leaves of Senecio teixeirae contain hepatotoxic pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which can be toxic in large quantities. However, in smaller amounts, the plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat conditions such as fever, wounds, and skin irritations. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be carefully considered due to its potential toxicity.
